title: F I G U R E 2 in Tag attachment innovation on spurdog (Squalus acanthias) reveals year-round coastal association of pregnant females in northeastern Atlantic waters
author: Junge, Claudia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: F I G U R E 2 Illustration of the spurdog (Squalus acanthias Linnaeus, 1758) tagging method. The attachment setup and positioning are shown in the main picture and the attachment steps are shown in sequence in the detail images: (a) threading of the 0.8-mm stainless-steel wire through the musculature and positioning of the first plate below the first dorsal fin, (b) positioning of the second plate and therefore closing of the harness to which the PSATs were tied, and (c) full setup shown from the top.
steps_raw: F I G U R E 2 Illustration of the spurdog (Squalus acanthias Linnaeus, 1758) tagging method. The attachment setup and positioning are shown in the main picture and the attachment steps are shown in sequence in the detail images: (a) threading of the 0.8-mm stainless-steel wire through the musculature and positioning of the first plate below the first dorsal fin, (b) positioning of the second plate and therefore closing of the harness to which the PSATs were tied, and (c) full setup shown from the top.
